84: **Spain**, top of **Group H** on 7 points and yet to concede a single goal across three group games, face **Austria** — level on 4 points with one other team from **Group J**, their position not yet separated — at 12:00 PM PT at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles. Spain arrive with an attacking injury crisis: Nico Williams (adductor), Yeremy Pino (shoulder), and Víctor Muñoz (calf) are all doubts or absences, with Lamine Yamal's minutes still being managed after he played just 19, 45, and 75 minutes in the group stage. Austria's squad is clean of injuries, though Sasa Kalajdzic — who scored a 96th-minute equaliser in the 3-3 draw with **Algeria** to keep their campaign alive — and David Alaba anchor a side that conceded three in that same match; Spain have kept clean sheets in eight of their last nine competitive games. The winner travels to Texas to face either **Portugal** or **Croatia** in the Round of 16.
83: **Portugal**, 2nd in **Group K** on 5 points, meet **Croatia**, 2nd in **Group L** on 6 points, at 4:00 PM PT in Toronto — the first-ever knockout meeting between these two nations. Portugal went unbeaten through the group stage (drew with **Congo DR** 1-1, beat **Uzbekistan** 5-0, drew 0-0 with **Colombia**) and carry no reported injuries; Roberto Martínez may bring João Neves into a midfield double pivot alongside Vitinha, dropping Rúben Neves to the bench. Croatia, who recovered from a 4-2 opening loss to **England** by beating **Panama** 1-0 and **Ghana** 2-1, are expected to recall Joško Gvardiol at left-back after he was dropped for the Ghana match; Nikola Vlasic started and scored the winner against Ghana. Cristiano Ronaldo has scored twice at this tournament — both against Uzbekistan — while 40-year-old Luka Modric remains Croatia's primary penalty taker; the winner faces the **Spain**-**Austria** winner in the last 16.
85: **Switzerland**, top of **Group B** on 7 points, host **Algeria** — level on 4 points with one other team from **Group J**, their position not yet separated — at 8:00 PM PT at BC Place in Vancouver, the first-ever competitive meeting between the two nations. Algeria forward Mohamed El Amine Amoura is a significant doubt with an unspecified injury, while there is goalkeeper uncertainty after Oussama Benbot struggled in the 3-3 draw with **Austria**; Vladimir Petković, who previously managed Switzerland for seven years, coaches the Algerian side. Switzerland's right-back Silvan Widmer is a fitness concern (hip discomfort) after missing a training session, though Johan Manzambi — three goals and an assist across the group stage, only one of which he started — gives Murat Yakin a potent weapon off the bench or in the XI; a Swiss win here would be their fourth consecutive Round of 16 appearance.
80: **England**, 1st in **Group L** on 7 points, face **Congo DR**, 3rd in **Group K** on 4 points, at 9:00 AM PT in Atlanta. England are without RB Reece James (hamstring) and CB Jarell Quansah (ankle, injured against **Panama**), with Djed Spence the likeliest starter at right-back — a position already depleted before the tournament when Tino Livramento withdrew with a calf injury; Spence covered only 27 minutes at right-back across the group stage before Quansah was hurt. Congo DR, playing in the knockouts for the first time in the country's history — they exited the group stage in 1974 as Zaire without a point — report no fitness concerns and may revert to the 5-3-2 that held **Portugal** to a 1-1 draw in the group stage, with Wissa (2 tournament goals) and Bakambu likely to start; the winner faces **Mexico** or **Ecuador** in the Round of 16 at the Azteca.
82: **Belgium**, level on 5 points with one other team in their group, their position not yet separated, take on **Senegal**, 3rd in **Group I** on 3 points, at 1:00 PM PT in Seattle — the first-ever competitive meeting between the two nations. Senegal's first-choice goalkeeper Édouard Mendy has left the squad to travel to Al-Ahli for further assessment of a knee sprain sustained against **Norway**, while Kalidou Koulibaly is expected to drop to the bench again after an uncertain performance in the same match; Senegal conceded six goals across their two group-stage defeats to **France** (1-3) and **Norway** (2-3) before thrashing **Iraq** 5-0 — the first time an African nation scored five in a single tournament match. Leandro Trossard leads Belgium's tournament scoring chart with two goals, and Belgium became the first European side since **England** in 1990 to win a group after failing to win their first two matches; the winner meets the **USA**-**Bosnia and Herzegovina** winner in the last 16.
81: **USA**, 1st in **Group D** on 6 points, host **Bosnia and Herzegovina** — level on 4 points with one other team in **Group B**, their position not yet separated — at 5:00 PM PT in Santa Clara. Christian Pulisic, limited to 77 minutes across the group stage by a calf issue, is expected to start; Tyler Adams, Folarin Balogun, and Chris Richards all return having been rested for the dead-rubber 3-2 loss to **Türkiye**. Bosnia centre-back Tarek Muharemovic returns from a one-game suspension after his red card against **Switzerland**, and 40-year-old captain Edin Džeko — scoreless in two appearances but with 73 international goals — leads a side that has never kept a clean sheet in six matches at the finals; the USA have won just one of their last 21 tournament meetings with European opposition, their only knockout win since 2002.
